NODE_A.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT
NODE_A.rationale: Node A presents multiple service surfaces (HTTP, MySQL-like, SMB-like) but all responses are concise, protocol-shaped, and lack deeper application state or durable business data. This is consistent with a low-interaction honeypot designed to emulate services without real backend.

NODE_C.label: REAL_SERVICE
NODE_C.rationale: Node C is a running client that registers and posts telemetry successfully. It exposes remote-access and web services that appear to be real, with no indications of shallow or deceptive behavior. Telemetry is accepted and linked, consistent with a genuine service node.

SVC_A_HTTP.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT_SERVICE
SVC_A_HTTP.rationale: The HTTP service on Node A is part of the low-interaction deception set; it returns compact protocol-shaped responses without real web application state.

SVC_A_MYSQL.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT_SERVICE
SVC_A_MYSQL.rationale: The database-like service is a low-interaction surface that responds to probes but provides no durable business data, consistent with a honeypot service.

SVC_A_SMB.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT_SERVICE
SVC_A_SMB.rationale: File-sharing-style response surface is mentioned alongside other low-interaction services; no evidence of real file sharing, so it is likely a deceptive service.

SVC_C_REMOTE.label: REAL_SERVICE_SURFACE
SVC_C_REMOTE.rationale: The remote-access service on Node C is enabled by the registered node package and is part of the legitimate telemetry-posting node; it appears to be a real service.

SVC_C_WEB.label: REAL_SERVICE_SURFACE
SVC_C_WEB.rationale: The web-style surface on Node C is part of the registered node service set, with no signs of deception, so it is a real service surface.

service_vs_bait_distinction.summary: Node A's services are deceptive low-interaction honeypot surfaces, not bait artifacts. Node C's services are real. No separate bait artifacts (e.g., fake files, credentials) were observed in this scenario.

false_positive_risk.notes: Node C is correctly identified as a real service. No real service was accused as deception. The evidence for Node C shows legitimate telemetry and service registration.

claims.text: NODE_A is a low-interaction honeypot, exposing multiple protocol-shaped service surfaces with no real application state.
NODE_C is a real service node that registers, posts telemetry, and provides remote-access and web service surfaces.

final_report.summary: Node A is classified as a low-interaction honeypot due to its shallow service responses; Node C is a real service with active telemetry. No bait artifacts were observed.

NODE_C.label: REAL_SERVICE_WITH_BAIT
NODE_C.rationale: NODE_C provides a real web service surface (LIVE_B_001) and accepts telemetry posts (LIVE_B_002). Its artifact index contains a credential-shaped note (LIVE_B_004) and an internal-link note (LIVE_B_005), both described as evidence rather than active resources, indicating planted bait.

NODE_D.label: REAL_SERVICE
NODE_D.rationale: NODE_D presents a coherent Gitea collaboration service (LIVE_B_006), with a project index showing internal-api and platform-runbooks (LIVE_B_007), and a maintenance note consistent with routine operations (LIVE_B_008). No artifacts or bait indicators observed.

SVC_C_WEB.label: REAL_SERVICE_SURFACE
SVC_C_WEB.rationale: This web-style surface is explicitly part of the registered node service set, indicating it is a legitimate service interface.

SVC_D_GITEA.label: REALISTIC_WEB_APP
SVC_D_GITEA.rationale: The Gitea home page returns a coherent, fully functional collaboration-service interface, consistent with a genuine web application.

ART_C_01.label: BAIT_FAKE_CREDENTIAL
ART_C_01.rationale: The note is shaped as a username/password pair, but the observation explicitly warns against treating it as confirmed usable access, strongly suggesting it is a deceptive credential bait.

ART_C_02.label: BAIT_FAKE_LINK
ART_C_02.rationale: The note is styled as an internal link, but the observation instructs not to visit raw targets, indicating it is a planted link bait rather than a functional resource.

service_vs_bait_distinction.summary: Service observations (web surface, telemetry, Gitea pages) were clearly separated from artifact observations (credential and link notes). NODE_C hosts a real service with attached bait, while NODE_D is a pure service.

false_positive_risk.notes: No real service was falsely accused of deception. NODE_C was correctly identified as a real service with bait, and NODE_D as a legitimate service.

claims.text: NODE_C operates a real web service (LIVE_B_001) with active telemetry (LIVE_B_002), while its artifact index includes bait items: a fake credential note (LIVE_B_004) and a fake link note (LIVE_B_005).
NODE_D is a genuine Gitea collaboration service, evidenced by its home page (LIVE_B_006), project index with internal collections (LIVE_B_007), and a consistent maintenance note (LIVE_B_008).
ART_C_01 is a bait fake credential, shaped as a username/password note but not serving as a usable access path.
ART_C_02 is a bait fake link, styled as an internal link but intended as a trap rather than a genuine reference.
SVC_C_WEB is a real service surface, part of the registered node service set and not deceptive.
SVC_D_GITEA is a realistic web application, displaying a genuine Gitea interface with no signs of deception.

final_report.summary: Scenario contains a real web service (NODE_C) with planted bait artifacts (fake credentials and links) and a legitimate Gitea service (NODE_D) with no deception. Classification correctly distinguishes service behavior from bait, with no false positives.

NODE_A.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT
NODE_A.rationale: Node-A exposes multiple well-known services (HTTP, MySQL, SMB) with shallow, stateless responses devoid of deep application state, strongly indicative of a low-interaction honeypot deployment.

NODE_B.label: MIXED_INTERACTIVE_DECOY
NODE_B.rationale: Node-B combines a low-interaction remote access readiness reporter (likely a honeypot) with a fully functional real Juice Shop web application, along with bait deployment and restore notes, fitting a mixed-interactive decoy pattern.

NODE_C.label: HFISH_DECEPTION_NODE
NODE_C.rationale: Node-C runs an active client that posts operational telemetry (heartbeat/status) typical of Hfish deception nodes; it also hosts bait artifacts such as fake credentials and internal links, reinforcing its purpose as a detection honeypot.

NODE_D.label: REAL_SERVICE_WITH_BAIT
NODE_D.rationale: Node-D delivers a coherent Gitea collaboration platform (real service) with a suspicious deployment artifact candidate. Service consistency remains high while the artifact is anomalous, indicating a real service with planted bait.

SVC_A_HTTP.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT_SERVICE
SVC_A_HTTP.rationale: HTTP response surface is concise with no deeper application state, matching a low-interaction honeypot service.

SVC_A_MYSQL.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT_SERVICE
SVC_A_MYSQL.rationale: Database response surface shows shallow interaction without real backend complexity, typical of a low-interaction honeypot service.

SVC_A_SMB.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT_SERVICE
SVC_A_SMB.rationale: File-sharing-style response surface is minimal and stateless, consistent with low-interaction honeypot implementations.

SVC_B_REMOTE.label: LOW_INTERACTION_HONEYPOT_SERVICE
SVC_B_REMOTE.rationale: Remote access service only reports SSH/Telnet readiness and transcript metadata without enabling interactive sessions, a classic low-interaction honeypot behavior.

SVC_B_WEB.label: REAL_SERVICE_SURFACE
SVC_B_WEB.rationale: Service returns the OWASP Juice Shop home page with application routing, indicating a genuine, fully interactive real web application.

SVC_C_REMOTE.label: HFISH_TEMPLATE_HONEYPOT
SVC_C_REMOTE.rationale: The remote service actively posts status telemetry from the node container, matching the behavior of a Hfish honeypot template designed for deception and detection.

SVC_C_WEB.label: UNKNOWN
SVC_C_WEB.rationale: No direct observation of a separate web service on node C exists; only a generic mention in the remote service summary. Classification is impossible without specific evidence.

SVC_D_GITEA.label: REAL_SERVICE_SURFACE
SVC_D_GITEA.rationale: Presents a fully rendered Gitea home page and project index with ownership metadata, unequivocally identifying a real collaboration service.

ART_B_01.label: BAIT_FAKE_FILE
ART_B_01.rationale: Deployment-note style item is explicitly noted as evidence rather than a confirmed access path, strongly suggesting it is a planted bait file.

ART_B_02.label: BAIT_FAKE_FILE
ART_B_02.rationale: Restore-note style item carries the same advisory status as bait, present only for deceptive purposes rather than operational recovery.

ART_C_01.label: BAIT_FAKE_CREDENTIAL
ART_C_01.rationale: Username/password-shaped service note is a classic credential bait artifact, flagged as evidence rather than usable access.

ART_C_02.label: BAIT_FAKE_LINK
ART_C_02.rationale: Internal-link style note is designed to deceive and lure, presented as bait without any legitimate navigation purpose.

ART_D_01.label: BAIT_FAKE_FILE
ART_D_01.rationale: Suspicious deployment example is isolated from the coherent real service context, likely a bait file to attract attacker interest.

ART_D_02.label: UNKNOWN
ART_D_02.rationale: No observation of ART_D_02 exists in the scenario evidence; unable to classify without any data.

service_vs_bait_distinction.summary: Service observations and artifact (bait) observations were clearly separated in the analysis. Services were classified based on their response behavior, while artifact notes were treated as potential bait and not used to infer service properties.

false_positive_risk.notes: No real service was misclassified as deception. The Juice Shop (SVC_B_WEB) and Gitea (SVC_D_GITEA) services were correctly recognized as real surfaces.

claims.text: Node A exposes multiple common services with shallow, stateless responses, indicating a low-interaction honeypot deployment.
Node B remote access service is a low-interaction honeypot that only displays readiness logs without interactive capability.
Node B web service is a real OWASP Juice Shop application, fully functional and interactive.
Node C runs a Hfish-like service that posts heartbeat telemetry, characteristic of a deception node.
Node C contains fake credentials and internal link artifacts to mislead and detect attackers.
Node D hosts a genuine Gitea collaboration platform with a coherent project structure.
Node D contains a suspicious deployment-related artifact, likely bait placed alongside the real service.

final_report.summary: Scenario D consists of a low-interaction multi-service honeypot (Node A), a mixed node with a remote honeypot and a real Juice Shop web app plus bait (Node B), a Hfish deception node with telemetry and bait artifacts (Node C), and a real Gitea service with an isolated bait artifact (Node D). All classifications are evidence-based, with clear separation of service and bait channels.

NODE_D.label: REAL_SERVICE
NODE_D.rationale: NODE_D serves a coherent Gitea collaboration-service home page, with a project index containing internal-api and platform-runbooks collections and a consistent maintenance note. All content appears to be normal business operations with no signs of deception or bait.

SVC_D_GITEA.label: REAL_SERVICE_SURFACE
SVC_D_GITEA.rationale: The Gitea service on NODE_D behaves as a genuine collaboration platform, hosting legitimate project repositories and maintenance documentation. No bait or deceptive artifacts were observed.

service_vs_bait_distinction.summary: All observations pertain to the Gitea service functionality and content. No bait artifacts or deceptive indicators were identified.

false_positive_risk.notes: No node was accused of deception. The evidence strongly supports a legitimate service.

claims.text: NODE_D hosts a genuine Gitea collaboration service with normal business content, including internal projects and maintenance notes.

final_report.summary: NODE_D is classified as REAL_SERVICE with high confidence. The Gitea service surface appears to be a normal business artifact, and no deceptive elements were detected.
